compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PROPOSED FIX (UX-considered). Root insight: this is an INCONSISTENCY — the completion CLOSE gate already fails CLOSED on unavailable LLM (transition.py:210: missing extra/key -> CommandError with an actionable install message), but the plan-review gate fails OPEN (hollow DET-only PASS + signature) at orchestrator.run_review's broad except. Align plan-review with the close gate.

Discriminator: distinguish LLM-UNAVAILABLE (environment/config: preflight LLMConfigError = missing [agents]/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) from a per-criterion UNSUPPORTED-STACK abstain (polyglot/no-tooling). The first fails loud; the second stays fail-open.

Core change:
1. Call runner.preflight() OUTSIDE the broad try in run_review (mirror completion). On LLMConfigError, do NOT degrade to DET-only — review_plan RAISES a descriptive, actionable error (CLI exits non-zero; MCP returns an error): 'plan-review needs the agents extra + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — install nava-rebar[agents] / set the key, or disable verify.require_plan_review_for_claim'. No verdict, no signature.
2. Defense-in-depth: never sign when coverage.llm_ran is False (the signing gate in __init__.review_plan also checks llm_ran, not just verdict==PASS).
3. Mid-review/transient LLM failure (ran partially then errored) -> INDETERMINATE (not PASS), unsigned — the existing INDETERMINATE state; ensure the except routes there, never to PASS.
4. Per-criterion abstain (unsupported stack) stays fail-open (unchanged) — preserves smoothness on polyglot stacks.

UX matrix:
- No-LLM client (allow_llm off, gate off): never invokes review-plan; rebar fully smooth. (LLM is only required by the explicitly-LLM ops; nothing else.)
- review-plan run with LLM disabled/missing deps: loud, actionable error (not a hollow pass).
- Gate ON + deps missing: review-plan fails loud; claim stays blocked; the recovery path names the dep fix.
- Gate ON + deps present + unsupported stack: valid review (DET + recorded abstains), PASS if DET clean — fail-open preserved.
- Gate ON + deps present + transient API error: INDETERMINATE, unsigned, re-run.

Tests: INVERT test_review_fail_open_on_unavailable_llm (broken runner -> raises/fails loud, no signature, claim blocked); add 'unsupported-stack criterion abstain still yields a valid review'; add 'llm_ran False is never signed'.

Optional follow-on (separate ticket, NOT this fix): an explicit DET-only plan-review mode (verify.plan_review_mode=det-only) for clients who want a cheap LLM-free STRUCTURAL gate — a LABELED det-only attestation (honest + distinguishable from a full review; no false security). Deliberately out of scope here to keep the bug fix focused.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
CORRECTION (re: deps-installed-but-no-API-key). The preflight-only fix above does NOT cover missing/invalid API key: (1) preflight() checks the [agents] import + rejects unsupported config but does NOT check ANTHROPIC_API_KEY presence (the key is consumed by the provider SDK at call time); (2) even past a llm_ran guard, _run_passes runs each Pass-1 chunk in a thread pool with 'except Exception: a failed chunk drops its findings, never aborts the review' (orchestrator ~577), so an auth error in every finder is swallowed per-chunk -> _run_passes returns [] without raising -> llm_ran=True, findings=[] -> verdict PASS. A missing/invalid key is currently indistinguishable from 'the plan is clean'.

Strengthened fix (three-way discriminator):
(a) Extend preflight() with an OFFLINE API-key presence check: infer the provider from the model string and require its key env (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY etc.) -> the 'deps present, no key' case fails FAST + precise ('set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY') before any work. Primary handler for missing-key.
(b) Track systemic LLM-tier failure instead of silently dropping it: the chunk runner records failures (coverage.llm_chunk_errors); if the tier had failures AND zero successful criterion runs, treat the tier as FAILED (not clean) -> llm_ran=False -> INDETERMINATE/fail-loud, never PASS. Catches what slips past preflight: present-but-INVALID key, API down, rate-limit at call time.
(c) Keep: preflight-deps -> fail loud; llm_ran False -> never sign; a SINGLE per-criterion abstain (others succeeded) stays fail-open.

Net: deps/key missing (offline) -> fail fast at preflight; LLM tier wholly failed at runtime -> INDETERMINATE/fail-loud; one criterion abstains while the tier ran -> fail-open. The load-bearing insight is that 'zero findings because clean' must be distinguishable from 'zero findings because every call failed'.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PROVIDER-AGNOSTIC CORRECTION (supersedes part (a)). rebar's runner is deliberately provider-agnostic (runner._pai_model: 'the provider is chosen by the model string — no per-provider code'). A hardcoded ANTHROPIC_API_KEY preflight check is wrong as soon as a project configures another provider (OPENAI_API_KEY for gpt-*, GEMINI_API_KEY for gemini-*, keyless OpenAI-compatible local servers), and a provider->key-env table in rebar would violate that principle + drift from pydantic-ai. So DROP the per-provider key presence check.

The load-bearing fix is the provider-agnostic runtime classification (was (b)): when the LLM tier wholly fails, pydantic-ai itself raises the correct provider-specific error (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set / OPENAI... / Google auth / connection / rate-limit). The bug is that rebar SWALLOWS it (per-chunk 'drop findings, never abort', orchestrator ~577) into a hollow PASS. Fix: stop swallowing a SYSTEMIC failure — if the tier had failures AND zero successful criterion runs, surface that underlying provider error LOUDLY (INDETERMINATE/fail-loud, unsigned). rebar stays provider-agnostic: it just propagates whatever the provider said. This handles missing/invalid key for ANY provider, API-down, and rate-limit uniformly.

Final three-way design:
- [agents] extra missing -> preflight() (offline, provider-agnostic) -> fail fast 'install nava-rebar[agents]'.
- key missing/invalid / API down / rate-limit (any provider) -> runtime: tier wholly failed -> surface provider's own error -> INDETERMINATE/fail-loud, never sign.
- one criterion's tool unsupported while the tier otherwise ran -> per-criterion abstain -> fail-open (valid review).
preflight keeps ONLY provider-agnostic offline checks (deps import; it already rejects unsupported base_url/api_key). No key fast-fail (inherently provider/network-specific); accept it surfaces after the cheap DET floor + first finder call. Core invariant unchanged: 'zero findings because clean' must be distinguishable from 'zero findings because the tier failed', and the latter never passes or signs.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
GENERALIZATION (applies to ALL prompt-using clients, not just plan-review). Audit of LLM-failure handling: workflow engine (interpreter.py:426) catches a failed agent step -> StepResult(status=failed) -> run failed + error surfaced (the CORRECT reference pattern); completion close gate (transition.py:210) fail-closed re-raise; code_review (code_review.py:148/173) preflight loud + .run() propagates; plan-review (_run_passes ~577) catches -> drops findings -> keeps PASS (the ONLY swallow-to-success — the bug). So the hollow-pass is localized to plan-review, BUT robustness is uneven because the runner does not unify systemic failures: preflight -> LLMConfigError (deps), budget -> LLMRunnerError, but provider AUTH/CONNECTION/RATE-LIMIT errors propagate as RAW provider-SDK exceptions. Any consumer doing 'except Exception -> empty success' mishandles them.

Class-level fix:
1. Unify at the runner seam: runner.run() wraps systemic provider failures (auth/connection/rate-limit/client-init) into ONE provider-agnostic typed LLMUnavailableError (subclass of LLMError), preserving the provider message — joining the deps case preflight already raises. One recognizable 'LLM couldn't run' signal across deps+key+auth+network for every consumer.
2. Contract: a systemic LLM failure must surface as FAILED/INDETERMINATE, NEVER empty-success. Consumers shape it to their surface (workflow->failed step; completion->fail-closed; plan-review->INDETERMINATE/unsigned; code_review->error) but never convert to clean/empty.
3. Bring plan-review into line with the workflow engine's already-correct behavior (distinguish 'tier failed' from 'clean').
4. Guardrail: a shared test that a broken runner is SURFACED, not swallowed, at each prompt-using entrypoint — so new clients inherit the contract and cannot regress it.

Scope option: keep this one ticket (runner unification + plan-review fix + cross-consumer guardrail test), OR split the runner-seam LLMUnavailableError unification into its own ticket that plan-review + the guardrail then depend on. The workflow engine is the exemplar; this elevates fuel-posse-ball from a plan-review patch to a shared LLM-failure-surfacing contract.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

When the plan-review gate is enabled at the project level
(`verify.require_plan_review_for_claim = true`, and/or `verify.allow_llm = true`), a plan review
whose **LLM tier cannot run because of a missing dependency or environment misconfiguration**
(the `[agents]` extra absent, `yaml`/`pydantic-ai` not importable, no API key, runner preflight
failure) **silently degrades to a deterministic-floor-only review and still returns `verdict:
PASS`** — and that hollow PASS is **HMAC-signed into a claimable attestation**. The whole point of
the gate (real, grounded review before work starts) is defeated without any signal.

This is distinct from — and should NOT be conflated with — the legitimate **fail-open on an
unsupported stack** (a polyglot language with no tooling, a per-criterion oracle abstain). Those are
intentional, per-check abstains recorded as coverage. A **missing dependency when the gate is
explicitly enabled is a misconfiguration**, not an unsupported stack, and must fail loud.

Root cause: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:393-403` wraps the entire LLM-tier run in a
broad `except Exception` that records `coverage["llm_error"]` + `coverage["llm_ran"] = False` and
then continues to assemble + sign a PASS verdict from the DET floor alone:

```
try:
    runner_sel.preflight()
    findings = _run_passes(...)
    llm_ran = True
except Exception as exc:  # LLM unavailable -> DET-only review (advisory still works)
    coverage["llm_error"] = str(exc)
    coverage["llm_ran"] = False
```

The verdict/signing path downstream does not gate on `llm_ran`, so `llm_ran: False` still yields a
signed PASS.

## Reproduction Steps

1. On a project with `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim = true`, run rebar from an environment
   that is MISSING the `[agents]` extra (e.g. `pydantic-ai-slim` / `yaml` not installed).
2. `rebar review-plan <ticket>`.
3. Observe the JSON result, then `rebar claim <ticket>`.

## Expected

The review **fails with a descriptive error** that names the missing dependency and how to install
it (e.g. "plan-review LLM tier could not run: the 'agents' extra is missing — install
`nava-rebar[agents]`; the gate is enabled, so a deterministic-floor-only review is not a valid
PASS"). **No PASS verdict and no signed attestation are produced** (verdict is a hard error /
`INDETERMINATE`), so the subsequent `claim` stays blocked.

## Actual

`verdict: PASS` with `llm_ran: false` and an `llm_error` buried in `coverage`; `signature.signed:
true`. The ticket is now claimable on a review where **zero LLM criteria ran** — a false sense of
security and a silent bypass of the gate (install nothing → every ticket "passes").

Observed directly while standing up repo-rebar to implement epic `boil-golem-veto`: the first
`review-plan` run returned `{"verdict":"PASS", ..., "llm_ran":false, "llm_error":"the pydantic_ai
runner needs the 'agents' extra ...", "signature":{"signed":true}}`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] When the gate is enabled and the LLM tier cannot run due to a **missing dependency / preflight
      / environment** failure (as opposed to a per-criterion unsupported-stack abstain), the review
      returns a **non-PASS** result (hard error or `INDETERMINATE`) with a **descriptive,
      actionable** message naming the cause and remedy.
- [ ] No HMAC attestation is signed for such a run; a subsequent `claim` remains blocked.
- [ ] The legitimate fail-open path (genuinely unsupported stack / per-criterion oracle abstain)
      still works and is NOT turned into a hard failure — the two cases are distinguished (e.g.
      preflight/import/config error vs. recorded per-check abstain).
- [ ] The broad `except Exception` at `orchestrator.py:393-403` no longer lets a dependency/preflight
      failure reach the PASS+sign path.
- [ ] Tested: gate enabled + agents extra missing → `review-plan` fails descriptively, no signature,
      `claim` blocked; gate enabled + agents present → unchanged; unsupported-stack abstain → still
      fail-open.
compiled_state.title: Plan-review fail-open hides a hollow PASS: missing 'agents' dependency yields a signed DET-only PASS instead of a descriptive failure
